steps.title: Define what an introduction requires
steps.content: The student wants to introduce the Slow Food movement to a new audience. A good introduction names the movement, establishes when it began, and conveys its core purpose — giving an unfamiliar reader enough to understand what it is and why it matters.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The signing of the "Slow Food Manifesto" in 1989 marked the founding of the international Slow Food movement, while the Slow Food USA organization was founded in 2000.’: lists two founding dates but says nothing about what the movement does or stands for — too thin to introduce it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The international Slow Food movement, founded in 1989, promotes universal access to healthy, high-quality food that is produced sustainably by workers who are treated and compensated fairly.’: names the movement, gives its founding year, and captures its three core aims (access, sustainability, fair treatment) in one sentence — a complete introduction. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The Slow Food movement advocates for food production workers.’: covers only one aim and omits the movement’s name, founding, and broader goals — far too narrow. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Goals of the movement include universal access to healthy, high-quality food and sustainable food practices.’: states two goals but never names the movement, making it useless as an introduction to a new audience. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
